project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

archived_sprint.name: Admin Dashboard Remaining Bug Fixes
archived_sprint.goal: Fix 4 remaining bugs from BUGS.md audit: CSV export memory issue (Bug 5), json-modal focus trap (Bug 13/14), and missing aria-labels on close buttons (Bug 15)

features.title: Stream CSV export to avoid unbounded memory accumulation
features.description: Bug 5: audit/export.ts accumulates ALL rows in `allRows[]` array before generating CSV string, potentially causing OOM with large datasets. Refactor to use streaming/incremental CSV generation instead of materializing entire dataset in memory.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user exports audit logs, when there are thousands of rows, then memory usage stays bounded
Given a user exports audit logs, when the export completes, then the CSV file is correctly generated with all rows
Given a user exports audit logs, when the export is truncated at MAX_ROWS, then truncation warning still displays correctly
features.technical_notes: Consider building CSV string incrementally per page (append rows to a growing string or use Blob parts array), avoiding holding both the full object array AND the full CSV string simultaneously. Keep the MAX_ROWS truncation limit and warning UI.

features.title: Add focus trap and Escape handler to json-modal.tsx
features.description: Bug 13/14: json-modal.tsx (full-screen JSON viewer) is the only remaining modal without focus trap and Escape key support. Tab key escapes to background elements and Escape does not close it.
acceptance_criteria: Given the JSON modal is open, when user presses Tab, then focus cycles within the modal only
Given the JSON modal is open, when user presses Escape, then the modal closes
Given the JSON modal is open, when it opens, then focus moves to the first focusable element inside the modal
features.technical_notes: Use existing `useFocusTrap` hook from `apps/admin/src/hooks/use-focus-trap.ts` — already used by all other modals (create-dialog, edit-sheet, key-display-dialog, revoke-dialog, detail-drawer). Follow the same pattern: pass a ref and onClose callback.

features.title: Add aria-label to all icon-only close buttons
features.description: Bug 15 (partial): Close (X) buttons in 4 modal/drawer components contain only an `<X>` icon with no `aria-label`, making them inaccessible to screen readers. Affected files: create-dialog.tsx, edit-sheet.tsx, detail-drawer.tsx, json-modal.tsx.
acceptance_criteria: Given a screen reader user encounters a close button, when the button is announced, then it reads a descriptive label like 'Close'
Given any modal/drawer with a close button, when inspected, then the <button> element has an aria-label attribute
features.technical_notes: Use i18n translation key for the aria-label (e.g., `t('common.close')`). Add the translation to both en and zh-CN locale files. Affected files: create-dialog.tsx (~line 74), edit-sheet.tsx (~line 69), detail-drawer.tsx (~line 251), json-modal.tsx (~line 222).

features.title: Update BUGS.md to reflect current fix status
features.description: After all bugs are fixed, update BUGS.md to mark all remaining bugs as fixed and add fix descriptions, so the bug tracking document is accurate.
acceptance_criteria: Given BUGS.md is read, when checking each bug, then all 18 bugs show correct fix status
Given BUGS.md is read, when checking the remaining bugs table, then no bugs remain unfixed
features.technical_notes: Move all remaining bugs (5, 13, 14, 15) to the 'Bugs Found & Fixed' section with fix descriptions. Update any summary text.
